title: Indiana Jones
content:
{{Top|ncl}} {{Otheruses|Jones}} {{Youmay|the character|the [[Indiana Jones (franchise)|franchise]]}} {{Noncanon|legends=1}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Wakeupnow.jpg]] |name=Indiana Jones |homeworld=[[Earth/Legends|Earth]] |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Star Wars: Yoda Stories]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]]{{Tales|19|Into the Great Unknown}} |feathers= |eyes=Hazel-Green |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=A [[Unidentified museum (Indiana Jones)|museum]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I'm Indiana Jones. Who are you, some kind of test pilot?|Indiana Jones, to Luke Skywalker|}} [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] '''Indiana Jones''' was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Archaeologist/Legends|archaeologist]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Earth/Legends|Earth]]. At one point in [[Time/Legends|time]], Jones was taken to the planet [[Xantar]] by the [[Unidentified Xantar species|alien]] "[[Bug-Eyes]]," where the doctor became frustrated with the lack of artifacts. "Bug-Eyes" later brought the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to the planet to serve as a friend to Jones. Skywalker instead suggested that the alien take Jones back to his home, which "Bug-Eyes" agreed to do. Later, between the Earth-years 1942 and 1943, Jones embarked on an expedition to find the legendary "Sasquatch" in a [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] on behalf of a [[Unidentified museum (Indiana Jones)|museum]]. Accompanied by his companion [[Short Round]] and a [[Indiana Jones's guide|guide]], the trio came across the wreck of the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Inside, Jones found the [[Death/Legends|remains]] of its [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and decided to cancel the search for the Sasquatch, which was actually Solo's co-pilot, the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. ==Biography== ===Xantar encounter=== {{Quote|Listen Bug-Eyes, you told me I'd find priceless artifacts, instead I'm slogging through an endless swamp!|Indiana Jones, to Bug-Eyes|}} [[File:IndianaJonesAndTheStoriesOfYoda.jpg|thumb|right|Indiana Jones meets Luke Skywalker]] Indiana Jones was a [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] and [[Archaeologist/Legends|archaeologist]] from the primitive [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Earth/Legends|Earth]]. At some point, his travels took him to the location [[Atlantis]]. On another occasion, Jones was taken by an [[Unidentified Xantar species|alien]] he called "[[Bug-Eyes]]" to the planet [[Xantar]] via [[Unidentified Xantar starship|starship]], with the promise that Jones would find priceless artifacts. On arrival, Jones instead grew frustrated navigating the planet's [[Swamp/Legends|swampy]] terrain, becoming lonely and depressed. Bug-Eyes tried to improve Jones' mood by finding him a companion, which the alien found in the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|trainee]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who Bug-Eyes brought to Xantar to meet Jones. As they landed, Bug-Eyes briefed Skywalker about Jones' disposition, at which point the archaeologist arrived complaining about his situation. Jones introduced himself to Skywalker, and asked if the Jedi was some kind of test [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], which led Skywalker to deduce that Jones came from a primitive world. The Jedi suggested that Bug-Eyes take Jones home, and the alien agreed, considering that a mistake had been made in taking Jones aboard. Prior to departing in the starship, Skywalker commented to Jones that the doctor resembled his friend, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. ===Expedition to the ''Millennium Falcon''=== {{Quote|What now, Doctor Jones? We continue looking for this 'monster'?''"
"''No. Let's leave him as part of the great unknown.|Short Round and Indiana Jones, discussing the "Sasquatch" Chewbacca|}} At some point in either of the [[Earth/Legends|Earth]]-years 1942 or 1943,{{Twitter|HadenBlackman|status/979049283197452293|[[W. Haden Blackman]]|quote=No - they are two different characters in my mind. We never put the story on any timelines b/c it is an Infinities tale, but the Indy portion needs to be 7 or 8 years after ToD|archivedate=20200812160832}} Jones was hired by a [[Unidentified museum (Indiana Jones)|museum]] to locate the legendary "Sasquatch," which had been sighted throughout the thirty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, in a [[Forest/Legends|forest]]. The Sasquatch was in reality the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], who had crash-landed in the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] with Solo 126 years earlier. Jones travelled to the jungle with his companion [[Short Round]] and a local [[Indiana Jones's guide|guide]]. [[File:Indyhan_2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Indiana Jones and Han Solo's remains]] As the trio approached a ridge where Chewbacca had previously been sighted, the guide pointed out their location and identified a set of footprints, which pleased Jones, although he commented that the museum was not paying only for footprints. Eventually, the group found the wreck of the ''Millennium Falcon'', which Jones climbed aboard first and proceeded to pull Short Round up onto the [[Starship/Legends|starship's]] ramp. Short Round asked Jones where they were, to which the doctor responded that he had no idea, as he had never seen its like, even in Atlantis. As he came across Solo's [[Death/Legends|corpse]] in the pilot's seat, Jones commented that he found the scene somehow familiar, and instructed Short Round to stay close. As Jones felt the fletching of one of the [[Arrow/Legends|arrows]] that had killed Solo, he expressed belief that the pilot's remains were Human and sympathized with his fate. Short Round then asked Jones if they would continue the search for the "monster," which Jones declined to do, believing that the mystery was best left part of the unknown. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|By the way, did anyone ever tell you that you look a lot like my friend Han Solo…?|Luke Skywalker, to Jones|}} Indiana Jones was usually out exploring to find lost relics.[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga: Prima Official Game Guide|''LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga'': Prima Official Game Guide]] In the opinion of Bug-Eyes, Indiana Jones was lonely, depressed and cynical. The doctor complained about the lack of artifacts on Xantar and the fact that he had to trawl through mud instead. Jones was hired by a museum to track down the "Sasquatch," but decided that the mysterious creature was better left alone after encountering the corpse of Han Solo. He had never seen anything resembling the ''Millennium Falcon'' before in his travels. Jones was a light-skinned human with [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and green [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], with, according to Luke Skywalker, a similar appearance to that of Solo. ==Equipment== Jones's typical [[Clothing/Legends|outfit]] included a brown [[Fedora/Legends|fedora]], a brown jacket, and a gray shirt. He also carried a [[Whip (weapon)|whip]] and a [[Slugthrower/Legends|pistol]], which made him a force to be reckoned with in combat. ==Behind the scenes== ===Overview=== {{Quote|Well, they usually ask me, ‘If there was a fight between Han Solo and Indiana Jones, who would fuckin’ win?’ And I say ''[voice rising, fingers drumming],'' ‘Me, asshole! I don’t want to fucking make shit up like that. I mean, what are you asking me that crap for?’|Harrison Ford, on fan questions about Indiana Jones and Han Solo|{{WebCite|url=https://www.esquire.com/entertainment/movies/a43965150/harrison-ford-indiana-jones-interview|text=Harrison Ford has Stories to Tell|author=D'agostino, Ryan|date=2023-05-31|work=[[Wikipedia:Esquire (magazine)|''Esquire'']]|archivedate=20230628153437}}}} [[File:Indiana_Jones_profile.png|thumb|right|250px|Harrison Ford as Indiana Jones in ''Raiders of the Lost Ark'']] Indiana Jones, born Henry Jones Jr.,[[w:c:indianajones:Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade|''Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade'']] is the central character of the eponymous [[Indiana Jones (franchise)|franchise]], beginning with the film [[Wikipedia:Raiders of the Lost Ark|''Raiders of the Lost Ark'']], which premiered on [[June 12]], [[1981]].''[[Star Wars Year by Year: A Visual Chronicle]]'' The character was conceived around [[1970]] by ''[[Star Wars]]'' creator [[George Lucas]], who wanted to recreate serial films from the [[1930s]]. Lucas mentioned the concept to his friend and fellow director [[Steven Spielberg]] when they were both vacationing in Hawaii on the weekend that [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] premiered in [[1977]], which led to Spielberg agreeing to direct the first film.''[[George Lucas: The Creative Impulse]]'' Lucas initially tried to avoid casting [[Harrison Ford]] as Indiana Jones, not wanting to be reliant on the actor after he appeared in Lucas's previous films [[Wikipedia:American Graffiti|''American Grafitti'']] and in ''Star Wars'' as Han Solo, but the actor was eventually cast.[[w:c:IndianaJones:Indiana Jones: Making the Trilogy|''Indiana Jones: Making the Trilogy'']] The first reference to Indiana Jones in a ''Star Wars'' source was the [[Bantha Tracks 9|ninth issue]] of the newsletter [[Bantha Tracks (newsletter)|''Bantha Tracks'']], published in the Summer of [[1980]]. The article did not mention the character by name, only announcing that Ford had been cast as the lead character in ''Raiders of the Lost Ark''.[[Bantha Tracks 9|''Bantha Tracks'' 9]] Jones was then included in posters for the film used in later issues of ''Bantha Tracks'', with the character's name not used in a ''Star Wars'' source until the [[Bantha Tracks 13|thirteenth issue]] of the magazine in [[August]] of 1981.[[Bantha Tracks 13|''Bantha Tracks'' 13]] ===''Star Wars'' media=== ====First references==== {{Quote|Greetings, Jones! We've been keeping an eye on you, and we've decided you deserve bigger challenges than you're getting around here. You'd be amazed at the wealth of artifacts just waiting for a man of your talents on Planet ''[''sic'']'' Xantar! We'd like you to lead our expedition, and we're ready to leave right now.''"
"''I don't want to go.''"
"''Sure you do, you'll love it there!|The exchange between "Bug-Eyes" and Indiana Jones, leading to the ''Star Wars'' crossover in ''Yoda Stories''|[[w:c:indianajones:Indiana Jones and his Desktop Adventures|''Indiana Jones and his Desktop Adventures'']]}} Indiana Jones has made numerous [[Canon|non-canon]] appearances throughout ''Star Wars'' media. His first non-magazine reference was made in [[1987]] in the rulebook for the first edition of [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']], which suggested that players could model character dynamics on the relationship between Jones and Short Round[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']] as portrayed in the second Indiana Jones film [[w:c:indianajones:Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom|''Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom'']] ([[1984]]).[[w:c:indianajones:Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom|''Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom'']] [[File:Indiana_Jones_Bug_Eyes.png|thumb|left|250px|Indiana Jones meets "Bug-Eyes" in ''Indiana Jones and his Desktop Adventures'' (pictured) before travelling to Xantar.]] Jones's first appearance in a ''Star Wars'' story was in the [[March]] [[1997]]{{LucasArtsCite|url=20th/history_3.htm|text=20th Anniversary History, Part Three, Broadening Horizons, 1995-1998}} [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Yoda Stories]]'', where the player controls Luke Skywalker and can encounter Jones and his alien abductor "Bug-Eyes" in one of the game's numerous possible endings. Skywalker comments on Jones' similar appearance to Solo, hinting at their shared portrayal by Harrison Ford. This appearance serves as a continuation of one of the endings of the [[1996]] LucasArts game [[w:c:indianajones:Indiana Jones and his Desktop Adventures|''Indiana Jones and his Desktop Adventures'']], in which "Bug-Eyes" meets Jones after he solves a puzzle while working in [[w:c:indianajones:Mexico|Mexico]]. The alien says that Jones deserves greater challenges than those currently available to him, and insists that the doctor lead an expedition on Xantar to find precious artifacts. Jones is reluctant to join, but relents after "Bug-Eyes" insistence and enters the alien's starship. In the novel ''[[The Paradise Snare]]'', the first entry in [[Star Wars: The Han Solo Trilogy|''The Han Solo Trilogy'']] by [[A. C. Crispin]], Solo uses the alias "[[Jenos Idanian]]," which is an anagram of Indiana Jones.''[[The Paradise Snare]]'' The novel was published on [[May 5]], [[1997]].{{Penguin|url=books/33592/the-paradise-snare-star-wars-legends-the-han-solo-trilogy-by-ac-crispin/9780553574159|text=The Paradise Snare: Star Wars Legends (The Han Solo Trilogy)}} {{C|Paperback}} ====Extended crossovers==== {{Quote|What is this place?''"
"''I don't know, I've never seen anything like it. Not even in Atlantis. But it's all somehow familiar. Stay close, Shorty.|Short Round and Indiana Jones, inside the ''Millennium Falcon''|}} Jones's next ''Star Wars'' appearance was in the crossover story "[[Into the Great Unknown]]," a comic written by [[W. Haden Blackman]], illustrated by [[Sean Gordon Murphy]], and published in the [[Star Wars Tales 19|nineteenth issue]] of ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[May 14]], [[2004]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53997/star_wars_tales_1999_19|text=Star Wars Tales (1999) #19}} The idea of a crossover between the two franchises had existed at Dark Horse prior to Blackman's involvement in writing the comic.{{WebCite|url=http://www.vanityfair.com/online/daily/2008/05/frank-digiaco-4.html|text=Frank DiGiacomo: Indiana Jones, Meet Han Solo|work=[[Wikipedia:Vanity Fair (magazine)|''Vanity Fair'']]|archivedate=20130114221823}} In it, Jones makes reference to his experience in [[w:c:indianajones:Atlantis|Atlantis]], a location he explored previously explored in the Dark Horse Comics series [[W:c:IndianaJones:Indiana Jones and the Fate of Atlantis (comic)|''Indiana Jones and the Fate of Atlantis'']],[[W:c:IndianaJones:Indiana Jones and the Fate of Atlantis (comic)|''Indiana Jones and the Fate of Atlantis'' comic]] the first issue of which was released on [[March 1]], [[1991]],{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/91-258/Indiana-Jones-and-the-the-Fate-of-Atlantis-1-of-4|text=Indiana Jones and the Fate of Atlantis #1 (of 4)}} and in the [[1992]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] of the [[W:c:IndianaJones:Indiana Jones and the Fate of Atlantis|same name]].[[W:c:IndianaJones:Indiana Jones and the Fate of Atlantis|''Indiana Jones and the Fate of Atlantis'']] Blackman later stated that the comic took place seven or eight years after ''Temple of Doom'', which occurs in 1935, placing "Into the Great Unknown" in either 1942 or 1943. [[File:Lego_Indiana_Jones.png|thumb|right|250px|Indiana Jones's LEGO profile]] Jones appeared as a playable character in the console versions of the [[2007]] [[Traveller's Tales]] video game ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]''. He can be unlocked in the room of the [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Mos Eisley Cantina]] adorned with the question mark symbol by entering the door with the camera image above it. There, the player can view the trailer for [[w:c:indianajones:LEGO Indiana Jones: The Original Adventures|''LEGO Indiana Jones: The Original Adventures'']], a then-upcoming video game that was released in [[2008]], and players will afterwards be able to purchase Jones to use as a playable character. In combat, Jones can fire with his pistol or whip enemies that are in close proximity.''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' Likewise, Solo is an unlockable character in ''LEGO Indiana Jones: The Original Adventures''.[[w:c:indianajones:LEGO Indiana Jones: The Original Adventures|''LEGO Indiana Jones: The Original Adventures'']] ====Continued cameos==== In the short film [[LEGO Star Wars: The Quest for R2-D2 (film)|''LEGO Star Wars: The Quest for R2-D2'']],[[LEGO Star Wars: The Quest for R2-D2 (film)|''LEGO Star Wars: The Quest for R2-D2'' film]] which first aired on [[August 28]], [[2009]],{{LEGOWebCite|subdomain=club|url=en-us/news/newsdetails.aspx?id=133077|text=LEGO® Star Wars™ TV Special On Cartoon Network Friday, August 28th!|nolive=1}} Jones can be seen searching through items at a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] garage sale, briefly holding the head of the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] before tossing it aside. In the [[Krome Studios]] video game ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Republic Heroes]]'',''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Republic Heroes]]'' which was first released on [[October 9]], 2009,{{OfficialBlog|url=2009/07/24/report-from-the-star-wars-spectacular-2009|text=Report from the ''Star Wars'' Spectacular 2009}} players can unlock "[[w:c:indianajones:Fedora|Indy's Hat]]" as a wearable item. [[File:Lego_Raiders.png|thumb|left|300px|Darth Vader watches Indiana Jones in ''LEGO Star Wars: Bombad Bounty'']] In the short film ''[[LEGO Star Wars: Bombad Bounty]]'', which premiered online on [[September 17]], [[2010]],{{OfficialYouTube|channel=channel/UCiRSlk1QpJMw5vFcq9-MW4w|name=LEGOClubTV|video=pPb2lIap6Es|text=LEGO® STAR WARS™: BOMBAD BOUNTY|archivedate=20101105225732}} [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] watches a [[LEGO]] recreation of ''Raiders of the Lost Ark'' on his television, particularly the scene where Jones steals the [[w:c:indianajones:Chachapoyan Fertility Idol|Chachapoyan Fertility Idol]] from the [[w:c:indianajones:Temple of the Chachapoyan Warriors|Temple of the Chachapoyan Warriors]] and evades the large boulder. He later appears on the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], where in the background, a scene of Solo running from a group of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] is replaced with Jones running away from the boulder.''[[LEGO Star Wars: Bombad Bounty]]'' Jones has two cameo appearances in the console versions ''[[LEGO Star Wars III: The Clone Wars]]'', which was released in [[2011]]. In free play mode of the level "The Hidden Enemy," the player can position two sports cars to be on top of two pads, which will reveal a tied-up Indiana Jones and his [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[Jones|Henry Jones, Sr.]],''[[LEGO Star Wars III: The Clone Wars]]'' in a recreation of a scene from the [[1989]] film [[w:c:indianajones:Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade|''Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade'']]. In the bonus level "Hostage Crisis," the player can connect a set of power cables to unlock a side-scrolling mini-game featuring Jones. The player has to step on a pad to cause Jones to jump across a series of chasms, after which they are awarded with a [[w:c:brickipedia:minikit|minikit]]. In the [[2012]] web game ''[[Star Wars Galactic Spy]]'', Jones has multiple appearances, along with other references to his franchise. He runs away from a boulder in the [[Mos Eisley Parade]], faces an [[w:c:indianajones:Arab Swordsman|Egyptian Swordsman]] in the [[Departure Lounge]], and fights two [[w:c:indianajones:Thuggee|Thuggee cultists]] in the [[Imperial garrison base]].{{SWArchive|url=play/online-games/galactic-spy|text=''Star Wars Galactic Spy''|int=Star Wars Galactic Spy}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Indiana Jones}} *{{SWArchive|url=play/online-games/galactic-spy|text=''Star Wars Galactic Spy''|int=Star Wars Galactic Spy}} *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Bombad Bounty]]'' *[[LEGO Star Wars: The Quest for Rinzler}} *{{InsiderCite|206|Launchpad}} *{{ThisWeek|HUO8Cvpn9b8|Indiana Jones is Back in Action, John Williams Performs, and More!|sw_url=video/indiana-jones-is-back-in-action-john-williams-performs-and-more}} *{{ThisWeek|HjvH1DrR5gY|Harrison Ford Reflects on Playing Indiana Jones, New Adventures Come to the Disney Parks, and More!|sw_url=video/harrison-ford-reflects-on-playing-indiana-jones-new-adventures-come-to-the-disney-parks-and-more}} *{{ThisWeek|zq1ArzOQc-o|This or Hat, Indiana Jones 101, and More!|sw_url=video/this-or-hat-indiana-jones-101-and-more}} *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-summer-viewing-guide|text=Beat the Tatooine Heat with StarWars.com's Summer Viewing Guide}} *{{ThisWeek|5BUi0UX9_o0|The Dial of Destiny Up Close, a Love Letter to Indiana Jones, and More!|sw_url=video/the-dial-of-destiny-up-close-a-love-letter-to-indiana-jones-and-more}} *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-best-of-2023|text=''Star Wars'': Best of 2023}} }} ==Notes and references== {{ScrollBox|content= {{Reflist}} }} ==External links== *{{WP|Indiana Jones (character)}} *{{IndyWiki|Indiana Jones}} *{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2012/03/endnotes-for-star-wars-book-of-sith.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for ''Star Wars: Book of Sith'' (part 1)}} *{{WebCite|url=http://www.indianajones.com/literature/news/news20040504.html|text=Indy Cameo in ''Star Wars Tales''|work=Indianajones.com|archivedate=20070702014018}} {{Interlang |es=Indiana Jones |ka=ინდიანა ჯოუნსი |pl=Indiana Jones }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Jones, Henry}} [[Category:Archaeologists]] [[Category:Crossover characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Star Wars culture]]
